what_input_feature: The 4 bytes at offset 0x10 must equal the FOURCC 'MCH3' (4d 43 48 33), the cmsSigMCH3Data colorspace signature read by _cmsLCMScolorSpace; loser inputs hold a different signature (e.g. 'RGB ' = 52 47 42 20).

source_line: case OP_Lt:               /* same as TK_LT, jump, in1, in3 */
what_input_feature: Input bytes must form a SQL statement that the parser accepts (a literal 'SELECT' keyword at offset 0) and that compiles to a VDBE program containing an OP_Lt opcode whose True (jump-dispatch) side is reached. The loser-blocking corpus is full of un-parseable byte runs; cmplog must repair the 'SELECT' keyword and surrounding token bytes so the bytecode reaches the comparison switch arm.
why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winning seeds all begin with the literal 'SELECT' (53 45 4c 45 43 54) followed by arithmetic/comparison expressions, e.g. seed 1 '53 45 4c 45 43 54 0d 35 2d 35 2f...' decoding to 'SELECT.5-5/55555' — a parseable SELECT body that the VDBE executes, reaching OP_Lt at L87187 (the blocker T side, hit 120 times in W).
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds are non-parseable: seed 1 is '69 3b 3b 3b...' ('i;;;;;;;') and seed 3 is 'd5 d5 d5 d5 5d 5d...', neither a valid SELECT, so the VDBE never builds/executes an OP_Lt opcode (L87187 shows T=0 for the loser).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts integer/string CMPs into a per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, then I2SRandReplace scans the input for bytes matching one CMP operand and substitutes the other — letting it install/repair the exact keyword and token bytes (e.g. the 'SELECT' lexeme compared inside the tokenizer) that naive's blind havoc cannot synthesize, so the VDBE compiles and dispatches the OP_Lt arm.

source_line: case OP_Lt:               /* same as TK_LT, jump, in1, in3 */
what_input_feature: Same program-side requirement: a parseable SELECT with a '<' comparison that compiles to and dispatches OP_Lt. The byte-level requirement is a multi-byte match against the parser's token comparisons (the 'SELECT' keyword and operator tokens), which value_profile reaches via incremental partial-prefix progress feedback.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile decisive subject resolves 10/10 with avg hitcount 506 on the branch vs the loser's 189; its corpus reaches OP_Lt (L87187 T side) because value_profile's CMP_MAP preserves inputs that get progressively closer to matching the parser's keyword/operator comparisons.
why_loser_doesnt: Naive's loser-blocking seeds never match the multi-byte keyword comparison (their offset-0 bytes are 'i', ' ', 'd5' rather than 'S'), and naive has no distance feedback to reward a near-match, so it stays blocked (8/10 blocked, branch True side T=0 for loser).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile writes each CMP into a CMP_MAP keyed by (PC, operand-distance bucket); an input whose operand pair is closer to matching than any seen before adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus. This Hamming/prefix gradient guides the corpus toward the exact 'SELECT'/operator bytes needed to compile an OP_Lt, which naive's edge-only feedback cannot reward.
